017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.lang.annotation.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.config.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.arg.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.converter.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.logger.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.openapi.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.staticfile.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.swagger.*;
042
043/**
044 * Used to denote that a class is a REST resource and to associate metadata on it.
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * Usually used on a subclass of {@link RestServlet}, but can be used to annotate any class that you want to expose as
048 * a REST resource.
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
051 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestAnnotatedClassBasics">@Rest-Annotated Class Basics</a>
052
053 * </ul>
054 */
055@Target(TYPE)
056@Retention(RUNTIME)
057@Inherited
058@AnnotationGroup(Rest.class)
059public @interface Rest {
060
061   /**
062    * Allowed header URL parameters.
063    *
064    * <p>
065    * When specified, allows headers such as <js>"Accept"</js> and <js>"Content-Type"</js> to be passed in as URL query
066    * parameters.
067    * <br>
068    * For example:
069    * <p class='burlenc'>
070    *  ?Accept=text/json&amp;Content-Type=text/json
071    * </p>
072    *
073    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
074    *    <li class='note'>
075    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
076    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
077    *    <li class='note'>
078    *       Use <js>"*"</js> to represent all methods.
079    *    <li class='note'>
080    *       Use <js>"NONE"</js> (case insensitive) to suppress inheriting a value from a parent class.
081    * </ul>
082    *
083    * @return The annotation value.
084    */
085   String allowedHeaderParams() default "";
086
087   /**
088    * Allowed method headers.
089    *
090    * <p>
091    * A comma-delimited list of HTTP method names that are allowed to be passed as values in an <c>X-Method</c> HTTP header
092    * to override the real HTTP method name.
093    * <p>
094    * Allows you to override the actual HTTP method with a simulated method.
095    * <br>For example, if an HTTP Client API doesn't support <c>PATCH</c> but does support <c>POST</c> (because
096    * <c>PATCH</c> is not part of the original HTTP spec), you can add a <c>X-Method: PATCH</c> header on a normal
097    * <c>HTTP POST /foo</c> request call which will make the HTTP call look like a <c>PATCH</c> request in any of the REST APIs.
098    *
099    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
100    *    <li class='note'>
101    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
102    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
103    *    <li class='note'>
104    *       Method names are case-insensitive.
105    *    <li class='note'>
106    *       Use <js>"*"</js> to represent all methods.
107    *    <li class='note'>
108    *       Use <js>"NONE"</js> (case insensitive) to suppress inheriting a value from a parent class.
109    * </ul>
110    *
111    * @return The annotation value.
112    */
113   String allowedMethodHeaders() default "";
114
115   /**
116    * Allowed method parameters.
117    *
118    * <p>
119    * When specified, the HTTP method can be overridden by passing in a <js>"method"</js> URL parameter on a regular
120    * GET request.
121    * <br>
122    * For example:
123    * <p class='burlenc'>
124    *  ?method=OPTIONS
125    * </p>
126    *
127    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
128    *    <li class='note'>
129    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
130    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
131    *    <li class='note'>
132    *       Use <js>"*"</js> to represent all methods.
133    *    <li class='note'>
134    *       Use <js>"NONE"</js> (case insensitive) to suppress inheriting a value from a parent class.
135    * </ul>
136    *
137    * @return The annotation value.
138    */
139   String allowedMethodParams() default "";
140
141   /**
142    * Specifies the logger to use for logging of HTTP requests and responses.
143    *
144    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
145    *    <li class='note'>
146    *       The default call logger if not specified is {@link CallLogger}.
147    *    <li class='note'>
148    *       The resource class itself will be used if it implements the {@link CallLogger} interface and not
149    *       explicitly overridden via this annotation.
150    *    <li class='note'>
151    *       The implementation must have one of the following constructors:
152    *       <ul>
153    *          <li><code><jk>public</jk> T(RestContext)</code>
154    *          <li><code><jk>public</jk> T()</code>
155    *          <li><code><jk>public static</jk> T <jsm>create</jsm>(RestContext)</code>
156    *          <li><code><jk>public static</jk> T <jsm>create</jsm>()</code>
157    *       </ul>
158    *    <li class='note'>
159    *       Inner classes of the REST resource class are allowed.
160    * </ul>
161    *
162    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
163    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerLoggingAndDebugging">Logging / Debugging</a>
164    * </ul>
165    *
166    * @return The annotation value.
167    */
168   Class<? extends CallLogger> callLogger() default CallLogger.Void.class;
169
170   /**
171    * Resource-level authenticator.
172    *
173    * <p>
174    * Resolves the {@link java.security.Principal} and roles for requests to this resource and its descendant child
175    * resources (inherited unless cut off via {@code noInherit={"authenticator"}}), so that {@code roleGuard} /
176    * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.RoleBasedRestGuard} / {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.Auth @Auth}
177    * / {@code isUserInRole} resolve against the computed identity.
178    *
179    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
180    *    <li class='note'>
181    *       The implementation is instantiated through the resource's bean store (constructor injection).
182    *    <li class='note'>
183    *       The default {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.RestAuthenticator.Null} sentinel means &quot;not specified.&quot;
184    * </ul>
185    *
186    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
187    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerAuthenticator">REST Authenticator</a>
188    * </ul>
189    *
190    * @return The annotation value.
191    * @since 10.0.0
192    */
193   Class<? extends RestAuthenticator> authenticator() default RestAuthenticator.Null.class;
194
195   /**
196    * REST children.
197    *
198    * <p>
199    * Defines children of this resource.  Each child resource is mounted under its own URL subtree (per the
200    * child's own {@code @Rest(path=...)} / {@code @Rest(paths=...)}) and constructs its own {@link RestContext}.
201    *
202    * <h5 class='section'>Children vs. mixins (resolution semantics)</h5>
203    * <p>
204    * Children are <b>isolated from the parent's resolution chain</b> by design — a child's serializers, parsers,
205    * guards, hooks, call-logger, etc. are all resolved against the child's own {@link RestContext} only, NOT walked
206    * through the parent.  This is the opposite of how {@link #mixins() mixins} resolve.  See the
207    * <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerMixinSubContexts#mixin-vs-child-divergence">Mixin Sub-Contexts &mdash; Mixin-vs-child divergence</a>
208    * topic for the rationale (children own their lifecycle and are externally mounted; mixins are inline composers
209    * sharing the host's URL namespace and resolution chain).
210    *
211    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
212    * <ul>
213    *    <li>Children on child are combined with those on parent class.
214    *    <li>Children are list parent-to-child in the order they appear in the annotation.
215    * </ul>
216    *
217    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
218    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ChildResources">Child Resources</a>
219    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerMixinSubContexts">Mixin Sub-Contexts</a> (for the mixin-vs-child divergence)
220    * </ul>
221    *
222    * @return The annotation value.
223    */
224   Class<?>[] children() default {};
225
226   /**
227    * REST mixins.
228    *
229    * <p>
230    * Defines operation-provider classes whose {@link RestOp @RestOp}-group methods should be composed into this
231    * resource.
232    *
233    * <p>
234    * Mixin methods are discovered the same way as local operation methods and surface under this resource's URL
235    * namespace.  On path/method collisions, local methods on this resource win over mixin methods.
236    *
237    * <h5 class='section'>Per-mixin RestContext + host-to-mixin inheritance (since 10.0.0)</h5>
238    * <p>
239    * Each mixin class is elevated to its own {@link RestContext} parent-linked to this host's {@link RestContext}.
240    * The mixin's class-level {@code @Rest(...)} configuration applies to its own endpoints, with inheritance from
241    * the host:
242    * <ul>
243    *    <li><b>List-shaped properties</b> ({@code serializers}, {@code parsers}, {@code encoders}, {@code converters},
244    *       {@code responseProcessors}, {@code restOpArgs}, {@code guards}) — host's chain runs first, then the mixin's
245    *       appended.  Host endpoints see only the host's chain.
246    *    <li><b>Replace-shaped properties</b> ({@code callLogger}, {@code debugEnablement}, {@code debugDefault},
247    *       {@code partSerializer}, {@code partParser}) — the mixin's value wins over the host's for mixin endpoints
248    *       when declared; otherwise the host's value is inherited.
249    *    <li><b>{@code messages}</b> — the mixin's bundle is chained as a child of the host's via
250    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.Messages#chain Messages.chain(child, parent)} so mixin keys win and missing
251    *       keys fall through to the host.
252    *    <li><b>Lifecycle hooks</b> ({@code @RestStartCall}, {@code @RestEndCall}, {@code @RestPreCall},
253    *       {@code @RestPostCall}, {@code @RestDestroy}) — dual-fire host-then-mixin for mixin-endpoint requests;
254    *       host-only for host-endpoint requests.
255    * </ul>
256    *
257    * <p>
258    * Use {@link #noInherit() @Rest(noInherit=&#123;...&#125;)} on a mixin class to cut off inheritance for a specific
259    * property — same token set as the host's {@code noInherit} machinery (e.g. {@code "serializers"},
260    * {@code "guards"}, {@code "messages"}).
261    *
262    * <h5 class='section'>Mixins vs. children</h5>
263    * <p>
264    * Mixins are inline composers that share the host's URL namespace and inherit from the host's resolution chain.
265    * {@link #children() Children} are independently-mounted resources isolated from the host's resolution chain.
266    * See the
267    * <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerMixinSubContexts#mixin-vs-child-divergence">Mixin Sub-Contexts &mdash; Mixin-vs-child divergence</a>
268    * topic for the rationale.
269    *
270    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
271    * <ul>
272    *    <li>Mixins on child are combined with those on parent class.
273    *    <li>Mixins are listed parent-to-child in the order they appear in the annotation.
274    *    <li>Transitive mixins ({@code A} mixes in {@code B}) parent-link flat to the host — both {@code A} and
275    *       {@code B} get {@code parentContext = host}, never an {@code A → B} chain.
276    * </ul>
277    *
278    * <p>
279    * For host-side per-mixin setting overrides (applying guards / serializers / a mount path / etc. to a
280    * specific mixin's endpoints without editing the mixin class), see the rich {@link #mixinDefs() mixinDefs}
281    * companion attribute.
282    *
283    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
284    *    <li class='jma'>{@link #mixinDefs()}
285    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerCompositionMixinsAndPaths">REST Server &mdash; Mixins and Multi-Mount Paths</a>
286    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerMixinSubContexts">REST Server &mdash; Mixin Sub-Contexts</a>
287    * </ul>
288    *
289    * @return The annotation value.
290    */
291   Class<?>[] mixins() default {};
292
293   /**
294    * Rich mixin definitions &mdash; the host-side override form of {@link #mixins()}.
295    *
296    * <p>
297    * Each {@link Mixin @Mixin} entry names a mixin class (via {@link Mixin#type()}) <b>and</b> lets the host
298    * override selected {@code @Rest}-level settings for that mixin's mixed-in endpoints &mdash; guards, role
299    * guards, serializers, default headers, mount path, etc. &mdash; without subclassing or editing the mixin
300    * class.
301    *
302    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
303    * <p class='bjava'>
304    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
305    *       mixinDefs=<ja>@Mixin</ja>(type=FooMixin.<jk>class</jk>, guards=AdminGuard.<jk>class</jk>)
306    *    )
307    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource { ... }
308    * </p>
309    *
310    * <p>
311    * This attribute is additive to {@link #mixins()}: bare-class entries in {@code mixins()} and rich entries
312    * here are discovered together (bare classes first, then {@code mixinDefs}).  A {@code @Mixin(type=X.class)}
313    * with no overrides is the exact equivalent of a bare {@code mixins=X.class} entry.
314    *
315    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
316    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Mixin}
317    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #mixins()}
318    * </ul>
319    *
320    * @return The annotation value.
321    * @since 10.0.0
322    */
323   Mixin[] mixinDefs() default {};
324
325   /**
326    * Client version header.
327    *
328    * <p>
329    * Specifies the name of the header used to denote the client version on HTTP requests.
330    *
331    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
332    *    <li class='note'>
333    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
334    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
335    * </ul>
336    *
337    * @return The annotation value.
338    */
339   String clientVersionHeader() default "";
340
341   /**
342    * Optional location of configuration file for this servlet.
343    *
344    * <p>
345    * The configuration file .
346    *
347    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
348    * <ul>
349    *    <li>Config file is searched for in child-to-parent order.
350    * </ul>
351    *
352    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
353    *    <li class='note'>
354    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
355    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
356    *    <li class='note'>
357    *       Use the keyword <c>SYSTEM_DEFAULT</c> to refer to the system default configuration
358    *       returned by the {@link Config#getSystemDefault()}.
359    * </ul>
360    *
361    * @return The annotation value.
362    */
363   String config() default "";
364
365   /**
366    * Eagerly initializes framework-managed memoizers during {@link RestContext} construction.
367    *
368    * <p>
369    * When enabled, framework bean memoizers and operation/child-context memoizers are force-fired inside the
370    * constructor try/catch so startup-time configuration errors fail fast.
371    *
372    * <p>
373    * This setting is disabled by default.
374    *
375    * <ul class='values'>
376    *    <li><js>"true"</js> - Force eager initialization during context construction.
377    *    <li><js>"false"</js> - Keep initialization lazy until first use (default).
378    * </ul>
379    *
380    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
381    *    <li class='note'>
382    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
383    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
384    * </ul>
385    *
386    * @return The annotation value.
387    */
388   String eagerInit() default "";
389
390   /**
391    * Opt this parent resource into deferred (first-invocation) construction of its {@link #children()} sub-resources.
392    *
393    * <p>
394    * When {@code "true"}, the {@link RestContext} instances for all {@code @Rest(children=...)} entries are
395    * <em>not</em> built at parent startup.  Instead, each child's routing entry is registered immediately (so URL
396    * matching is fully operational from the first request), but the full {@link RestContext} — including all of its
397    * bean-store setup, memoizers, and lifecycle hooks — is constructed on the first inbound request to that child's
398    * URL prefix.  Subsequent requests reuse the already-built context.
399    *
400    * <p>
401    * This is particularly useful when a parent resource exposes heavyweight admin or diagnostic children that are
402    * rarely invoked in production.  Setting {@code lazyChildren="true"} on the parent lets the parent boot fast;
403    * each child pays its construction cost only when (and if) it is first accessed.
404    *
405    * <ul class='values'>
406    *    <li><js>"true"</js> - Children are constructed on first invocation (deferred).
407    *    <li><js>"false"</js> (default) - Children are constructed eagerly at parent startup.
408    * </ul>
409    *
410    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
411    *    <li class='note'>
412    *       The first request to a lazy child pays the full construction cost, which can be significant for
413    *       heavyweight children.  If predictable first-request latency is required, do not opt in.
414    *    <li class='note'>
415    *       Concurrent first-requests to the same lazy child are serialized: only one thread runs the construction;
416    *       others block until it completes.
417    *    <li class='note'>
418    *       A lazy child that is never invoked is never constructed.  Its lifecycle {@code @RestDestroy} / shutdown
419    *       hooks are skipped at parent destruction time.
420    *    <li class='note'>
421    *       The programmatic knob {@link RestContext.Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)} overrides this annotation.
422    *    <li class='note'>
423    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
424    *       (e.g. <js>"$E{LAZY_CHILDREN,false}"</js>).
425    * </ul>
426    *
427    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
428    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestContext.Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)}
429    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #children()}
430    * </ul>
431    *
432    * @return The annotation value.
433    */
434   String lazyChildren() default "";
435
436   /**
437    * Supported content media types.
438    *
439    * <p>
440    * Overrides the media types inferred from the parsers that identify what media types can be consumed by the resource.
441    *
442    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
443    *    <li class='note'>
444    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
445    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
446    * </ul>
447    *
448    * @return The annotation value.
449    */
450   String[] consumes() default {};
451
452   /**
453    * Allowed serializer session option keys for this resource (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
454    *
455    * <p>
456    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys (e.g. <js>"escapeSolidus,maxIndent"</js>) that clients may
457    * send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Serializer-Options"</js> header or <js>"juneauSerializerOptions"</js> query parameter.
458    * Keys not in the effective allowlist cause a {@code 400 Bad Request} response.
459    *
460    * <p>
461    * Entries are merged in application order. A leading hyphen removes a previously added key: <js>"-escapeSolidus"</js>.
462    * Method-level {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestGet#allowedSerializerOptions()} values are always merged on top.
463    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to prevent inheriting less-derived contributions.
464    *
465    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
466    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
467    * </ul>
468    *
469    * @return The annotation value.
470    */
471   String[] allowedSerializerOptions() default {};
472
473   /**
474    * Allowed parser session option keys for this resource (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
475    *
476    * <p>
477    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Parser-Options"</js>
478    * header or <js>"juneauParserOptions"</js> query parameter. Parser options are ignored for operations without
479    * a request-body parser. Keys not in the effective allowlist cause a {@code 400 Bad Request} response.
480    *
481    * <p>
482    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to prevent inheriting less-derived contributions.
483    *
484    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
485    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
486    * </ul>
487    *
488    * @return The annotation value.
489    */
490   String[] allowedParserOptions() default {};
491
492   /**
493    * Property names for which less-derived contributions are NOT inherited.
494    *
495    * <p>
496    * Accepted values include {@code "allowedParserOptions"}, {@code "allowedSerializerOptions"},
497    * {@code "allowedHeaderParams"}, {@code "allowedMethodHeaders"}, {@code "allowedMethodParams"},
498    * {@code "disableContentParam"}, {@code "renderResponseStackTraces"}, {@code "problemDetails"},
499    * {@code "eagerInit"}, {@code "lazyChildren"}, {@code "clientVersionHeader"},
500    * {@code "uriAuthority"}, {@code "uriContext"}, {@code "uriRelativity"}, and {@code "uriResolution"}.
501    * Each entry is SVL-resolved then comma-split. Prevents the named property from inheriting values from
502    * parent {@code @Rest} annotations (router hierarchy). The {@code noInherit} attribute itself is never inherited.
503    *
504    * <h5 class='section'>Mixin sub-contexts (since 10.0.0)</h5>
505    * <p>
506    * On a class declared via {@link #mixins() @Rest(mixins=...)} on a host, {@code noInherit} also blocks the
507    * host-to-mixin inheritance walk for the named property.  The token set extends to every contribution list
508    * exposed by {@code @Rest}: {@code "serializers"}, {@code "parsers"}, {@code "encoders"}, {@code "converters"},
509    * {@code "responseProcessors"}, {@code "restOpArgs"}, {@code "guards"}, {@code "callLogger"},
510    * {@code "debugEnablement"}, {@code "debugDefault"}, {@code "partSerializer"}, {@code "partParser"}, and
511    * {@code "messages"}.  For example, {@code @Rest(noInherit={"guards"})} on a mixin removes the host's guard
512    * chain from the mixin's endpoints (typical pattern for deliberately-unguarded probes like
513    * {@code HealthMixin}).
514    *
515    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
516    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions">Session Options</a>
517    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerMixinSubContexts">REST Server &mdash; Mixin Sub-Contexts</a> (for the per-property opt-out semantics)
518    * </ul>
519    *
520    * @return The annotation value.
521    */
522   String[] noInherit() default {};
523
524   /**
525    * Class-level response converters.
526    *
527    * <p>
528    * Associates one or more {@link RestConverter converters} with a resource class.
529    *
530    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
531    * <ul>
532    *    <li>Converters on child are combined with those on parent class.
533    *    <li>Converters are executed child-to-parent in the order they appear in the annotation.
534    *    <li>Converters on methods are executed before those on classes.
535    * </ul>
536    *
537    * @return The annotation value.
538    */
539   Class<? extends RestConverter>[] converters() default {};
540
541   /**
542    * Enable debug mode.
543    *
544    * <p>
545    * Enables the following:
546    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
547    *    <li>
548    *       HTTP request/response bodies are cached in memory for logging purposes.
549    *    <li>
550    *       HTTP requests/responses are logged to the registered {@link CallLogger}.
551    * </ul>
552    *
553    * <ul class='values'>
554    *    <li><js>"true"</js> - Debug is enabled for all requests.
555    *    <li><js>"false"</js> - Debug is disabled for all requests.
556    *    <li><js>"conditional"</js> - Debug is enabled only for requests that have a <c class='snippet'>Debug: true</c> header.
557    * </ul>
558    *
559    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
560    *    <li class='note'>
561    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
562    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
563    *    <li class='note'>
564    *       These debug settings can be overridden by the {@link Rest#debug()} annotation or at runtime by directly
565    *       calling {@link RestRequest#setDebug()}.
566    * </ul>
567    *
568    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
569    *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestContext#getDebugEnablement()}
570    * </ul>
571    *
572    * @return The annotation value.
573    */
574   Debug debug() default @Debug;
575
576   /**
577    * Default <c>Accept</c> header.
578    *
579    * <p>
580    * The default value for the <c>Accept</c> header if not specified on a request.
581    *
582    * <p>
583    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
584    *
585    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
586    *    <li class='note'>
587    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
588    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
589    * </ul>
590    *
591    * @return The annotation value.
592    */
593   String defaultAccept() default "";
594
595   /**
596    * Default character encoding.
597    *
598    * <p>
599    * The default character encoding for the request and response if not specified on the request.
600    *
601    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
602    *    <li class='note'>
603    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
604    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
605    * </ul>
606    *
607    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
608    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#defaultCharset}
609    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestGet#defaultCharset}
610    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestPut#defaultCharset}
611    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestPost#defaultCharset}
612    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestDelete#defaultCharset}
613    * </ul>
614    *
615    * @return The annotation value.
616    */
617   String defaultCharset() default "";
618
619   /**
620    * Default <c>Content-Type</c> header.
621    *
622    * <p>
623    * The default value for the <c>Content-Type</c> header if not specified on a request.
624    *
625    * <p>
626    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
627    *
628    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
629    *    <li class='note'>
630    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
631    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
632    * </ul>
633    *
634    * @return The annotation value.
635    */
636   String defaultContentType() default "";
637
638   /**
639    * Default request attributes.
640    *
641    * <p>
642    * Specifies default values for request attributes if they're not already set on the request.
643    *
644    * <p>
645    * Affects values returned by the following methods:
646    *    <ul>
647    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttribute(String)}.
648    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttributes()}.
649    *    </ul>
650    *
651    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
652    * <p class='bjava'>
653    *    <jc>// Defined via annotation resolving to a config file setting with default value.</jc>
654    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo=bar"</js>, <js>"Baz: $C{REST/myAttributeValue}"</js>})
655    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
656    *
657    *       <jc>// Override at the method level.</jc>
658    *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo: bar"</js>})
659    *       <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod() {...}
660    *    }
661    * </p>
662    *
663    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
664    *    <li class='note'>
665    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
666    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
667    * </ul>
668    *
669    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
670    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#defaultRequestAttributes()}
671    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestGet#defaultRequestAttributes()}
672    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestPut#defaultRequestAttributes()}
673    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestPost#defaultRequestAttributes()}
674    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestDelete#defaultRequestAttributes()}
675    * </ul>
676    *
677    * @return The annotation value.
678    */
679   String[] defaultRequestAttributes() default {};
680
681   /**
682    * Default request headers.
683    *
684    * <p>
685    * Specifies default values for request headers if they're not passed in through the request.
686    *
687    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
688    *    <li class='note'>
689    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
690    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
691    * </ul>
692    *
693    * @return The annotation value.
694    */
695   String[] defaultRequestHeaders() default {};
696
697   /**
698    * Default response headers.
699    *
700    * <p>
701    * Specifies default values for response headers if they're not set after the Java REST method is called.
702    *
703    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
704    *    <li class='note'>
705    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
706    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
707    * </ul>
708    *
709    * @return The annotation value.
710    */
711   String[] defaultResponseHeaders() default {};
712
713   /**
714    * Optional servlet description.
715    *
716    * <p>
717    * It is used to populate the Swagger description field.
718    *
719    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
720    * <ul>
721    *    <li>Description is searched for in child-to-parent order.
722    * </ul>
723    *
724    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
725    *    <li class='note'>
726    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
727    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
728    *    <li class='note'>
729    *       The format is plain-text.
730    *       <br>Multiple lines are concatenated with newlines.
731    * </ul>
732    *
733    * @return The annotation value.
734    */
735   String[] description() default {};
736
737   /**
738    * Disable content URL parameter.
739    *
740    * <p>
741    * When enabled, the HTTP content content on PUT and POST requests can be passed in as text using the <js>"content"</js>
742    * URL parameter.
743    * <br>
744    * For example:
745    * <p class='burlenc'>
746    *  ?content=(name='John%20Smith',age=45)
747    * </p>
748    *
749    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
750    *    <li class='note'>
751    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
752    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
753    * </ul>
754    *
755    * @return The annotation value.
756    */
757   String disableContentParam() default "";
758
759   /**
760    * Opt the resource into <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807">RFC 7807</a> /
761    * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457">RFC 9457</a>
762    * {@code application/problem+json} error responses.
763    *
764    * <p>
765    * When enabled, the resource:
766    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
767    *    <li>Emits {@code application/problem+json} for thrown {@code BasicHttpException}s &mdash; regardless of the
768    *       client's {@code Accept} header. The body is a serialized
769    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.Problem} populated from the exception's status code, reason phrase,
770    *       and message.
771    *    <li>Honors the client's {@code Accept} header on the success path. When a {@code @RestOp} method returns a
772    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.Problem} (or throws a
773    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.ProblemException}), the
774    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.ProblemDetailsProcessor} only serializes it as
775    *       {@code application/problem+json} when the {@code Accept} header matches that media type (or {@code *&#47;*}).
776    * </ul>
777    *
778    * <ul class='values'>
779    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; enable problem-details responses for this resource.
780    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; disable problem-details responses for this resource (overrides an inherited
781    *       {@code "true"} from a parent {@code @Rest}).
782    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherit from the next-most-derived {@code @Rest} in the resource-class
783    *       hierarchy. Default behavior (no opt-in anywhere in the chain) is unchanged from prior releases.
784    * </ul>
785    *
786    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
787    *    <li class='note'>
788    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
789    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
790    *    <li class='note'>
791    *       Use {@link #noInherit()} to prevent inheriting an opt-in from a parent {@code @Rest}.
792    * </ul>
793    *
794    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
795    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.Problem}
796    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807.ProblemException}
797    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.ProblemDetailsProcessor}
798    * </ul>
799    *
800    * @return The annotation value.
801    */
802   String problemDetails() default "";
803
804   /**
805    * Opt this resource into per-request virtual-thread dispatch (Java 21+).
806    *
807    * <p>
808    * When enabled, every {@code @RestOp}-annotated handler invocation on this resource is submitted to a
809    * {@code Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor()} virtual-thread-per-task executor
810    * lazily built by the {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestContext}. The platform request thread blocks on
811    * the virtual thread's completion (so the handler's return value, exceptions, and observability hooks are
812    * preserved verbatim), but blocking I/O inside the handler now parks a virtual thread instead of the
813    * carrier — i.e. the carrier thread is freed to service other concurrent requests while the handler is
814    * parked on socket / file / lock waits. Combined with {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture}
815    * return types ({@code @RestGet} / {@code @RestPost} returning {@code CompletableFuture<T>}) this is the
816    * high-throughput pattern.
817    *
818    * <p>
819    * <b>Graceful degradation on Java 17/18/19/20:</b> the flag is detected during {@code RestContext}
820    * initialization. If the runtime is older than Java 21, a one-shot {@code WARNING} is logged and the
821    * resource falls back to the standard caller-thread dispatch path — no runtime error.
822    *
823    * <p>
824    * Per-{@code @RestOp} overrides are available via {@link RestOp#virtualThreads()}; the op-level setting
825    * takes precedence over the resource-level setting.
826    *
827    * <ul class='values'>
828    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; enable virtual-thread dispatch on Java 21+ (silently disabled on older JVMs).
829    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; explicitly disable.
830    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherit from the next-most-derived {@code @Rest} in the resource-class
831    *       hierarchy.
832    * </ul>
833    *
834    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
835    *    <li class='note'>
836    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
837    *       (e.g. <js>"$E{ENABLE_VIRTUAL_THREADS,false}"</js>).
838    *    <li class='note'>
839    *       Synchronized blocks and JNI calls in handler code <i>pin</i> a virtual thread to its carrier thread
840    *       — prefer {@link java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantLock} over {@code synchronized} in handlers run
841    *       under this flag.
842    * </ul>
843    *
844    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
845    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#virtualThreads()}
846    * </ul>
847    *
848    * @return The annotation value.
849    */
850   String virtualThreads() default "";
851
852   /**
853    * Per-resource observability opt-in / opt-out control.
854    *
855    * <p>
856    * Controls whether the observability block ({@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.MetricsRecorder} /
857    * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.TracerHook}) fires for operations on this resource.
858    * Per-operation overrides are available via {@link RestOp#observability()} (and the verb annotations).
859    *
860    * <ul class='values'>
861    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; strict opt-in: the resource <em>requires</em> a wired observability backend.
862    *       If neither a {@code @Bean MetricsRecorder} nor a {@code @Bean TracerHook} is registered when the
863    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestContext} is built, construction fails with a precise error.
864    *       All operations on this resource have observability enabled.
865    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; explicit opt-out: the observability block is short-circuited for every
866    *       operation on this resource, even when a wired backend is present. Also suppresses
867    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.TraceContextResponseProcessor} header injection for this resource.
868    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherits the existing behavior: the observability block runs per-op,
869    *       but a missing {@code @Bean} silently falls back to the NoOp singleton — no startup error.
870    * </ul>
871    *
872    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
873    *    <li class='note'>
874    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
875    *       (e.g. <js>"$E{ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY,false}"</js>).
876    *    <li class='note'>
877    *       Per-operation overrides via {@link RestOp#observability()} (or verb annotations) take precedence over
878    *       this resource-level setting.
879    * </ul>
880    *
881    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
882    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#observability()}
883    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.MetricsRecorder}
884    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.TracerHook}
885    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerObservability">REST Server &mdash; Observability</a>
886    * </ul>
887    *
888    * @return The annotation value.
889    * @since 10.0.0
890    */
891   String observability() default "";
892
893   /**
894    * Configurable timeout (milliseconds) applied to {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture}-returning
895    * handlers by {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.AsyncResponseProcessor}. Default is 30,000 ms.
896    *
897    * <p>
898    * On timeout, the future is cancelled with {@code mayInterruptIfRunning=true} and the response is
899    * committed as {@code 504 Gateway Timeout}. Set to {@code "0"} to disable the timeout entirely.
900    *
901    * <p>
902    * Per-{@code @RestOp} overrides are available via {@link RestOp#asyncTimeoutMillis()}; the op-level
903    * setting takes precedence over the resource-level setting.
904    *
905    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
906    *    <li class='note'>
907    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
908    *       (e.g. <js>"$E{ASYNC_TIMEOUT_MS,30000}"</js>).
909    * </ul>
910    *
911    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
912    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.AsyncResponseProcessor}
913    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#asyncTimeoutMillis()}
914    * </ul>
915    *
916    * @return The annotation value.
917    * @since 10.0.0
918    */
919   String asyncTimeoutMillis() default "";
920
921   /**
922    * Names the {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} bean used to route {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture}
923    * completion callbacks through a dedicated thread pool.
924    *
925    * <p>
926    * When set, the {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.AsyncResponseProcessor} switches from
927    * {@code future.whenComplete(callback)} to {@code future.whenCompleteAsync(callback, executor)}, so the
928    * response-handler work runs on the named pool instead of the future's natural completion thread (often an
929    * I/O-driver or database-callback thread).
930    *
931    * <p>
932    * The value is the {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Bean#name() @Bean(name=...)} lookup key for an
933    * {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} registered in the resource's bean store.  Empty string (default)
934    * means no override — completion callbacks run on the future's natural thread (the existing behavior).
935    *
936    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
937    * <p class='bjava'>
938    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(path=<js>"/api"</js>, asyncCompletionExecutor=<js>"myCompletionPool"</js>)
939    *    <jk>public class</jk> ApiResource <jk>extends</jk> RestServlet {
940    *
941    *       <ja>@Bean</ja>(name=<js>"myCompletionPool"</js>)
942    *       <jk>public</jk> Executor completionPool() {
943    *          <jk>return</jk> Executors.newFixedThreadPool(8);
944    *       }
945    *
946    *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/orders/{id}"</js>)
947    *       <jk>public</jk> CompletableFuture&lt;Order&gt; getOrder(<ja>@Path</ja> String id) {
948    *          <jk>return</jk> orderService.fetchAsync(id); <jc>// callback runs on myCompletionPool</jc>
949    *       }
950    *    }
951    * </p>
952    *
953    * <p>
954    * Per-operation overrides are available via {@link RestOp#asyncCompletionExecutor()} (and the verb annotations).
955    * A reference to a bean name that does not resolve in the bean store causes a startup failure.
956    *
957    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
958    *    <li class='note'>
959    *       MDC propagation still works when this executor is set — the
960    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.MdcAsyncListener} wraps the callback <em>before</em> it is
961    *       routed through the executor, so the MDC snapshot is restored on whichever thread the executor picks.
962    * </ul>
963    *
964    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
965    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.AsyncResponseProcessor}
966    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#asyncCompletionExecutor()}
967    * </ul>
968    *
969    * @return The annotation value.
970    * @since 10.0.0
971    */
972   String asyncCompletionExecutor() default "";
973
974   /**
975    * Specifies the compression encoders for this resource.
976    *
977    * <p>
978    * Encoders are used to enable various kinds of compression (e.g. <js>"gzip"</js>) on requests and responses.
979    *
980    * <p>
981    * Encoders are automatically inherited from {@link Rest#encoders()} annotations on parent classes with the encoders on child classes
982    * prepended to the encoder group.
983    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.EncoderSet.NoInherit} class can be used to prevent inheriting from the parent class.
984    *
985    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
986    * <p class='bjava'>
987    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that handles GZIP compression.</jc>
988    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
989    *       encoders={
990    *          GzipEncoder.<jk>class</jk>
991    *       }
992    *    )
993    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
994    *       ...
995    *    }
996    * </p>
997    *
998    * <p>
999    * The encoders can also be tailored at the method level using {@link RestOp#encoders()} (and related annotations).
1000    *
1001    * <p>
1002    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute an {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.EncoderSet} bean via
1003    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Bean @Bean(name="encoders")}.
1004    *
1005    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
1006    * <ul>
1007    *    <li>Encoders on child are combined with those on parent class.
1008    * </ul>
1009    *
1010    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1011    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerEncoders">Encoders</a>
1012    * </ul>
1013    *
1014    * @return The annotation value.
1015    */
1016   Class<? extends Encoder>[] encoders() default {};
1017
1018   /**
1019    * Default form data parameter definitions.
1020    *
1021    * <p>
1022    * Provides default values for {@link FormData @FormData} annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class.
1023    *
1024    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1025    * <p class='bjava'>
1026    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1027    *       formDataParams={
1028    *          <ja>@FormData</ja>(name=<js>"action"</js>, def=<js>"submit"</js>)
1029    *       }
1030    *    )
1031    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {...}
1032    * </p>
1033    *
1034    * @return The annotation value.
1035    * @since 9.2.0
1036    */
1037   FormData[] formDataParams() default {};
1038
1039   /**
1040    * Class-level guards.
1041    *
1042    * <p>
1043    * Associates one or more {@link RestGuard RestGuards} with all REST methods defined in this class.
1044    *
1045    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
1046    * <ul>
1047    *    <li>Guards on child are combined with those on parent class.
1048    *    <li>Guards are executed child-to-parent in the order they appear in the annotation.
1049    *    <li>Guards on methods are executed before those on classes.
1050    * </ul>
1051    *
1052    * @return The annotation value.
1053    */
1054   Class<? extends RestGuard>[] guards() default {};
1055
1056   /**
1057    * Default header parameter definitions.
1058    *
1059    * <p>
1060    * Provides default values for {@link Header @Header} annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class.
1061    *
1062    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1063    * <p class='bjava'>
1064    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1065    *       headerParams={
1066    *          <ja>@Header</ja>(name=<js>"Accept-Language"</js>, def=<js>"en-US"</js>),
1067    *          <ja>@Header</ja>(name=<js>"X-API-Version"</js>, def=<js>"1.0"</js>)
1068    *       }
1069    *    )
1070    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {...}
1071    * </p>
1072    *
1073    * @return The annotation value.
1074    * @since 9.2.0
1075    */
1076   Header[] headerParams() default {};
1077
1078   /**
1079    * The maximum allowed input size (in bytes) on HTTP requests.
1080    *
1081    * <p>
1082    * Useful for alleviating DoS attacks by throwing an exception when too much input is received instead of resulting
1083    * in out-of-memory errors which could affect system stability.
1084    *
1085    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1086    *    <li class='note'>
1087    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1088    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1089    * </ul>
1090    *
1091    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1092    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#maxInput}
1093    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestPost#maxInput}
1094    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestPut#maxInput}
1095    * </ul>
1096    *
1097    * @return The annotation value.
1098    */
1099   String maxInput() default "";
1100
1101   /**
1102    * Messages.
1103    *
1104    * Identifies the location of the resource bundle for this class.
1105    *
1106    * <p>
1107    * There are two possible formats:
1108    * <ul>
1109    *    <li>A simple string - Represents the {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.Messages.Builder#name(String) name} of the resource bundle.
1110    *       <br><br><i>Example:</i>
1111    *       <p class='bjava'>
1112    *    <jc>// Bundle name is Messages.properties.</jc>
1113    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(messages=<js>"Messages"</js>)
1114    *       </p>
1115    *    <li>Simplified JSON - Represents parameters for the {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.Messages.Builder} class.
1116    *       <br><br><i>Example:</i>
1117    *       <p class='bjava'>
1118    *    <jc>// Bundles can be found in two packages.</jc>
1119    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(messages=<js>"{name:'Messages',baseNames:['{package}.{name}','{package}.i18n.{name}']"</js>)
1120    *       </p>
1121    * </ul>
1122    *
1123    * <p>
1124    * If the bundle name is not specified, the class name of the resource object is used.
1125    *
1126    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1127    *    <li class='note'>
1128    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1129    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1130    * </ul>
1131    *
1132    * @return The annotation value.
1133    */
1134   String messages() default "";
1135
1136   /**
1137    * Specifies the parsers for converting HTTP request bodies into POJOs.
1138    *
1139    * <p>
1140    * Parsers are used to convert the content of HTTP requests into POJOs.
1141    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework parsers can be used in this setting.
1142    * <br>The parser selected is based on the request <c>Content-Type</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
1143    * using a best-match algorithm:
1144    * <ul class='javatree'>
1145    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Parser#getMediaTypes()}
1146    * </ul>
1147    *
1148    * <p>
1149    * Parsers are automatically inherited from {@link Rest#parsers()} annotations on parent classes with the parsers on child classes
1150    * prepended to the parser group.
1151    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet.NoInherit} class can be used to prevent inheriting from the parent class.
1152    *
1153    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1154    * <p class='bjava'>
1155    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can consume JSON and XML.</jc>
1156    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1157    *       parsers={
1158    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
1159    *          XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
1160    *       }
1161    *    )
1162    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
1163    *       ...
1164    *    }
1165    * </p>
1166    *
1167    * <p>
1168    * The parsers can also be tailored at the method level using {@link RestOp#parsers()} (and related annotations).
1169    *
1170    * <p>
1171    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet} bean via
1172    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Bean @Bean(name="parsers")}.
1173    *
1174    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
1175    * <ul>
1176    *    <li>Parsers on child override those on parent class.
1177    *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet.Inherit} class can be used to inherit and augment values from parent.
1178    *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet.NoInherit} class can be used to suppress inheriting from parent.
1179    *    <li>Parsers on methods take precedence over those on classes.
1180    * </ul>
1181    *
1182    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1183    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
1184    * </ul>
1185    *
1186    * @return The annotation value.
1187    */
1188   Class<?>[] parsers() default {};
1189
1190   /**
1191    * HTTP part parser.
1192    *
1193    * <p>
1194    * Specifies the {@link HttpPartParser} to use for parsing headers, query/form parameters, and URI parts.
1195    *
1196    * @return The annotation value.
1197    */
1198   Class<? extends HttpPartParser> partParser() default HttpPartParser.Void.class;
1199
1200   /**
1201    * HTTP part serializer.
1202    *
1203    * <p>
1204    * Specifies the {@link HttpPartSerializer} to use for serializing headers, query/form parameters, and URI parts.
1205    *
1206    * @return The annotation value.
1207    */
1208   Class<? extends HttpPartSerializer> partSerializer() default HttpPartSerializer.Void.class;
1209
1210   /**
1211    * Resource path.
1212    *
1213    * <p>
1214    * Used in the following situations:
1215    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1216    *    <li>
1217    *       On child resources (resource classes attached to parents via the {@link #children()} annotation) to identify
1218    *       the subpath used to access the child resource relative to the parent.
1219    *    <li>
1220    *       On top-level {@link RestServlet} classes deployed as Spring beans when <c>JuneauRestInitializer</c> is being used.
1221    * </ul>
1222    *
1223    * <h5 class='topic'>On child resources</h5>
1224    * <p>
1225    * The typical usage is to define a path to a child resource relative to the parent resource.
1226    *
1227    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
1228    * <p class='bjava'>
1229    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1230    *       children={ChildResource.<jk>class</jk>}
1231    *    )
1232    *    <jk>public class</jk> TopLevelResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {...}
1233    *
1234    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1235    *    path=<js>"/child"</js>,
1236    *    children={GrandchildResource.<jk>class</jk>}
1237    * )
1238    * <jk>public class</jk> ChildResource {...}
1239    *
1240    * <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1241    *    path=<js>"/grandchild"</js>
1242    * )
1243    * <jk>public class</jk> GrandchildResource {
1244    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/"</js>)
1245    *    <jk>public</jk> String sayHello() {
1246    *       <jk>return</jk> <js>"Hello!"</js>;
1247    *    }
1248    * }
1249    * </p>
1250    * <p>
1251    * In the example above, assuming the <c>TopLevelResource</c> servlet is deployed to path <c>/myContext/myServlet</c>,
1252    * then the <c>sayHello</c> method is accessible through the URI <c>/myContext/myServlet/child/grandchild</c>.
1253    *
1254    * <p>
1255    * Note that in this scenario, the <c>path</c> attribute is not defined on the top-level resource.
1256    * Specifying the path on the top-level resource has no effect, but can be used for readability purposes.
1257    *
1258    * <h5 class='topic'>Path variables</h5>
1259    * <p>
1260    * The path can contain variables that get resolved to {@link org.apache.juneau.http.Path @Path} parameters
1261    * or access through the {@link RestRequest#getPathParams()} method.
1262    *
1263    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
1264    * <p class='bjava'>
1265    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1266    *       path=<js>"/myResource/{foo}/{bar}"</js>
1267    *    )
1268    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
1269    *
1270    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/{baz}"</js>)
1271    *    <jk>public void</jk> String doX(<ja>@Path</ja> String <jv>foo</jv>, <ja>@Path</ja> <jk>int</jk> <jv>bar</jv>, <ja>@Path</ja> MyPojo <jv>baz</jv>) {
1272    *       ...
1273    *    }
1274    *    }
1275    * </p>
1276    *
1277    * <p>
1278    * Variables can be used on either top-level or child resources and can be defined on multiple levels.
1279    *
1280    * <p>
1281    * All variables in the path must be specified or else the target will not resolve and a <c>404</c> will result.
1282    *
1283    * <p>
1284    * When variables are used on a path of a top-level resource deployed as a Spring bean in a Spring Boot application,
1285    * the first part of the URL must be a literal which will be used as the servlet path of the registered servlet.
1286    *
1287    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1288    *    <li class='note'>
1289    *       The leading slash is optional.  <js>"/myResource"</js> and <js>"myResource"</js> is equivalent.
1290    *    <li class='note'>
1291    *       The paths <js>"/myResource"</js> and <js>"/myResource/*"</js> are equivalent.
1292    *    <li class='note'>
1293    *       Paths must not end with <js>"/"</js> (per the servlet spec).
1294    *    <li class='note'>
1295    *       Effective only when this class is registered directly with the servlet container as a top-level
1296    *       resource.  When this class is imported as a mixin via {@code @Rest(mixins=...)}, the importing
1297    *       host's own {@code path()} or {@link #paths()} governs the mount and this annotation is silently
1298    *       ignored &mdash; mixin endpoints land in the host's URL namespace.
1299    * </ul>
1300    *
1301    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
1302    * <ul>
1303    *    <li>Path is searched for in child-to-parent order.
1304    * </ul>
1305    *
1306    * @return The annotation value.
1307    */
1308   String path() default "";
1309
1310   /**
1311    * Additional servlet mount paths.
1312    *
1313    * <p>
1314    * Optional multi-mount companion to {@link #path()} for top-level servlet deployment.
1315    *
1316    * <p>
1317    * When specified, servlet containers may mount this resource on each listed path.
1318    * This is primarily intended for built-in support endpoints such as health probes where multiple exact URLs
1319    * should be served by a single servlet instance.
1320    *
1321    * <h5 class='section'>Runtime substitution (since 10.0.0)</h5>
1322    * <p>
1323    * Each array element is treated as a template:
1324    * <ol>
1325    *    <li>The element runs through
1326    *       <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL</a>
1327    *       variable substitution (e.g. {@code $C{health.paths}}, {@code $E{HEALTH_PATHS,/healthz}},
1328    *       {@code $S{my.system.prop,/healthz}}) using the bootstrap {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.VarResolver
1329    *       VarResolver} on the bean store.
1330    *    <li>The post-SVL value is split on {@code ,}; each piece is trimmed and empty pieces are dropped.
1331    *       A single template element can therefore expand to zero, one, or many mount paths.
1332    * </ol>
1333    *
1334    * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
1335    * <p class='bjava'>
1336    *    <jc>// 1. Single key resolving to a multi-value config string.</jc>
1337    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(paths={<js>"$C{health.paths}"</js>})
1338    *
1339    *    <jc>// 2. Mix literal + resolved element.</jc>
1340    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(paths={<js>"/api"</js>, <js>"$C{extra.paths}"</js>})
1341    *
1342    *    <jc>// 3. Env var with comma-separated defaults baked in.</jc>
1343    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(paths={<js>"$E{HEALTH_PATHS,/healthz,/readyz}"</js>})
1344    * </p>
1345    *
1346    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1347    *    <li class='note'>
1348    *       Paths are normalized to servlet path-specs by the hosting runtime.
1349    *    <li class='note'>
1350    *       When both {@link #path()} and {@link #paths()} are present, runtimes may use {@link #paths()} for
1351    *       top-level mounting and continue using {@link #path()} for child-resource composition.
1352    *    <li class='note'>
1353    *       The annotation default sits at the lowest rung of the runtime-override resolution chain &mdash; see
1354    *       {@link RestContext#getPaths()} for the full precedence order (programmatic &gt; getter &gt; annotation).
1355    *    <li class='note'>
1356    *       An SVL failure (unresolved variable with no default) falls back to the literal element rather than
1357    *       throwing during construction.
1358    *    <li class='note'>
1359    *       Effective only when this class is registered directly with the servlet container as a top-level
1360    *       resource.  When this class is imported as a mixin via {@code @Rest(mixins=...)}, the importing
1361    *       host's own {@link #path()} or {@code paths()} governs the mount and this annotation is silently
1362    *       ignored &mdash; mixin endpoints land in the host's URL namespace.
1363    * </ul>
1364    *
1365    * @return The annotation value.
1366    */
1367   String[] paths() default {};
1368
1369   /**
1370    * Default path parameter definitions.
1371    *
1372    * <p>
1373    * Provides default values for {@link Path @Path} annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class.
1374    *
1375    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1376    * <p class='bjava'>
1377    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1378    *       pathParams={
1379    *          <ja>@Path</ja>(name=<js>"version"</js>, def=<js>"v1"</js>)
1380    *       }
1381    *    )
1382    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {...}
1383    * </p>
1384    *
1385    * @return The annotation value.
1386    * @since 9.2.0
1387    */
1388   Path[] pathParams() default {};
1389
1390   /**
1391    * Supported accept media types.
1392    *
1393    * <p>
1394    * Overrides the media types inferred from the serializers that identify what media types can be produced by the resource.
1395    *
1396    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1397    *    <li class='note'>
1398    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1399    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1400    * </ul>
1401    *
1402    * @return The annotation value.
1403    */
1404   String[] produces() default {};
1405
1406   /**
1407    * Default query parameter definitions.
1408    *
1409    * <p>
1410    * Provides default values for {@link Query @Query} annotations on method parameters across all methods in this class.
1411    * Values specified here are used as defaults when the same property is not explicitly defined on a method parameter's
1412    * {@link Query @Query} annotation.
1413    *
1414    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1415    * <p class='bjava'>
1416    *    <jc>// Define common query parameters at class level</jc>
1417    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1418    *       queryParams={
1419    *          <ja>@Query</ja>(name=<js>"format"</js>, def=<js>"json"</js>, description=<js>"Output format"</js>),
1420    *          <ja>@Query</ja>(name=<js>"verbose"</js>, def=<js>"false"</js>, schema=<ja>@Schema</ja>(type=<js>"boolean"</js>))
1421    *       }
1422    *    )
1423    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
1424    *
1425    *       <jc>// Method will inherit the default query parameter definitions</jc>
1426    *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>
1427    *       <jk>public</jk> String doGet(<ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"format"</js>) String format, <ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"verbose"</js>) <jk>boolean</jk> verbose) {...}
1428    *
1429    *       <jc>// Can override defaults on a per-method basis</jc>
1430    *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>
1431    *       <jk>public</jk> String doGet2(<ja>@Query</ja>(name=<js>"format"</js>, def=<js>"xml"</js>) String format) {...}
1432    *    }
1433    * </p>
1434    *
1435    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1436    *    <li class='note'>
1437    *       These defaults apply to validation, parsing, and OpenAPI/Swagger documentation generation.
1438    *    <li class='note'>
1439    *       Method-level {@link Query @Query} annotations take precedence over class-level definitions.
1440    *    <li class='note'>
1441    *       The {@link Query#name() name} attribute must be specified for each query definition.
1442    * </ul>
1443    *
1444    * @return The annotation value.
1445    * @since 9.2.0
1446    */
1447   Query[] queryParams() default {};
1448
1449   /**
1450    * Render response stack traces in responses.
1451    *
1452    * <p>
1453    * Render stack traces in HTTP response bodies when errors occur.
1454    *
1455    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1456    *    <li class='note'>
1457    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1458    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1459    * </ul>
1460    *
1461    * @return The annotation value.
1462    */
1463   String renderResponseStackTraces() default "";
1464
1465   /**
1466    * Response processors.
1467    *
1468    * <p>
1469    * Specifies a list of {@link ResponseProcessor} classes that know how to convert POJOs returned by REST methods or
1470    * set via {@link RestResponse#setContent(Object)} into appropriate HTTP responses.
1471    *
1472    * @return The annotation value.
1473    */
1474   Class<? extends ResponseProcessor>[] responseProcessors() default {};
1475
1476   /**
1477    * Java REST operation method parameter resolvers.
1478    *
1479    * <p>
1480    * By default, the Juneau framework will automatically Java method parameters of various types (e.g.
1481    * <c>RestRequest</c>, <c>Accept</c>, <c>Reader</c>).
1482    * <br>This setting allows you to provide your own resolvers for your own class types that you want resolved.
1483    *
1484    * @return The annotation value.
1485    */
1486   Class<? extends RestOpArg>[] restOpArgs() default {};
1487
1488   /**
1489    * Role guard.
1490    *
1491    * <p>
1492    * An expression defining if a user with the specified roles are allowed to access methods on this class.
1493    *
1494    * <p>
1495    * This is a shortcut for specifying {@link RestOp#roleGuard()} on all the REST operations on a class.
1496    *
1497    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1498    * <p class='bjava'>
1499    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1500    *       path=<js>"/foo"</js>,
1501    *       roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
1502    *    )
1503    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
1504    *       ...
1505    *    }
1506    * </p>
1507    *
1508    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1509    *    <li class='note'>
1510    *       Supports any of the following expression constructs:
1511    *       <ul>
1512    *          <li><js>"foo"</js> - Single arguments.
1513    *          <li><js>"foo,bar,baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments.
1514    *          <li><js>"foo | bar | baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, pipe syntax.
1515    *          <li><js>"foo || bar || baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, Java-OR syntax.
1516    *          <li><js>"fo*"</js> - Patterns including <js>'*'</js> and <js>'?'</js>.
1517    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, ampersand syntax.
1518    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp;&amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, Java-AND syntax.
1519    *          <li><js>"fo* || (*oo || bar)"</js> - Parenthesis.
1520    *       </ul>
1521    *    <li>
1522    *       AND operations take precedence over OR operations (as expected).
1523    *    <li>
1524    *       Whitespace is ignored.
1525    *    <li>
1526    *       <jk>null</jk> or empty expressions always match as <jk>false</jk>.
1527    *    <li>
1528    *       If patterns are used, you must specify the list of declared roles using {@link #rolesDeclared()}.
1529    *    <li>
1530    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1531    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1532    * </ul>
1533    *
1534    * @return The annotation value.
1535    */
1536   String roleGuard() default "";
1537
1538   /**
1539    * Declared roles.
1540    *
1541    * <p>
1542    * A comma-delimited list of all possible user roles.
1543    *
1544    * <p>
1545    * Used in conjunction with {@link #roleGuard()} is used with patterns.
1546    *
1547    * <p>
1548    * This is a shortcut for specifying {@link RestOp#rolesDeclared()} on all the REST operations on a class.
1549    *
1550    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1551    * <p class='bjava'>
1552    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1553    *       rolesDeclared=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN,ROLE_READ_WRITE,ROLE_READ_ONLY,ROLE_SPECIAL"</js>,
1554    *       roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
1555    *    )
1556    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
1557    *       ...
1558    *    }
1559    * </p>
1560    *
1561    * @return The annotation value.
1562    */
1563   String rolesDeclared() default "";
1564
1565   /**
1566    * Specifies the serializers for POJOs into HTTP response bodies.
1567    *
1568    * <p>
1569    * Serializer are used to convert POJOs to HTTP response bodies.
1570    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework serializers can be used in this setting.
1571    * <br>The serializer selected is based on the request <c>Accept</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
1572    * using a best-match algorithm:
1573    * <ul class='javatree'>
1574    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Serializer#getMediaTypeRanges()}
1575    * </ul>
1576    *
1577    * <p>
1578    * Serializers are automatically inherited from {@link Rest#serializers()} annotations on parent classes with the serializers on child classes
1579    * prepended to the serializer group.
1580    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet.NoInherit} class can be used to prevent inheriting from the parent class.
1581    *
1582    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1583    * <p class='bjava'>
1584    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can produce JSON and XML.</jc>
1585    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1586    *       serializers={
1587    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
1588    *          XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
1589    *       }
1590    *    )
1591    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
1592    *       ...
1593    *    }
1594    * </p>
1595    *
1596    * <p>
1597    * The serializers can also be tailored at the method level using {@link RestOp#serializers()} (and related annotations).
1598    *
1599    * <p>
1600    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet} bean via
1601    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Bean @Bean(name="serializers")}.
1602    *
1603    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
1604    * <ul>
1605    *    <li>Serializers on child override those on parent class.
1606    *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet.Inherit} class can be used to inherit and augment values from parent.
1607    *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet.NoInherit} class can be used to suppress inheriting from parent.
1608    *    <li>Serializers on methods take precedence over those on classes.
1609    * </ul>
1610    *
1611    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1612    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
1613    * </ul>
1614    *
1615    * @return The annotation value.
1616    */
1617   Class<? extends Serializer>[] serializers() default {};
1618
1619   /**
1620    * Optional site name.
1621    *
1622    * <p>
1623    * The site name is intended to be a title that can be applied to the entire site.
1624    *
1625    * <p>
1626    * One possible use is if you want to add the same title to the top of all pages by defining a header on a
1627    * common parent class like so:
1628    * <p class='bjava'>
1629    *  <ja>@HtmlDocConfig</ja>(
1630    *       header={
1631    *          <js>"&lt;h1&gt;$RS{siteName}&lt;/h1&gt;"</js>,
1632    *          <js>"&lt;h2&gt;$RS{title}&lt;/h2&gt;"</js>
1633    *       }
1634    *    )
1635    * </p>
1636    *
1637    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1638    *    <li class='note'>
1639    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1640    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1641    * </ul>
1642    *
1643    * @return The annotation value.
1644    */
1645   String siteName() default "";
1646
1647   /**
1648    * Static files.
1649    *
1650    * <p>
1651    * Used to retrieve localized files to be served up as static files through the REST API via the following
1652    * predefined methods:
1653    * <ul class='javatree'>
1654    *    <li class='jm'>{@code BasicRestResource.getHtdoc(String, Locale)}.
1655    *    <li class='jm'>{@code BasicRestServlet.getHtdoc(String, Locale)}.
1656    * </ul>
1657    *
1658    * <p>
1659    * The static file finder can be accessed through the following methods:
1660    * <ul class='javatree'>
1661    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestContext#getStaticFiles()}
1662    * </ul>
1663    *
1664    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
1665    * <ul>
1666    *    <li>Static files on child are combined with those on parent class.
1667    *    <li>Static files are are executed child-to-parent in the order they appear in the annotation.
1668    * </ul>
1669    *
1670    * @return The annotation value.
1671    */
1672   Class<? extends StaticFiles> staticFiles() default StaticFiles.Void.class;
1673
1674   /**
1675    * Provides swagger-specific metadata on this resource.
1676    *
1677    * <p>
1678    * Used to populate the auto-generated OPTIONS swagger documentation.
1679    *
1680    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1681    * <p class='bjava'>
1682    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1683    *       path=<js>"/addressBook"</js>,
1684    *
1685    *       <jc>// Swagger info.</jc>
1686    *       swagger=@Swagger({
1687    *          <js>"contact:{name:'John Smith',email:'john@smith.com'},"</js>,
1688    *          <js>"license:{name:'Apache 2.0',url:'http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html'},"</js>,
1689    *          <js>"version:'2.0',</js>,
1690    *          <js>"termsOfService:'You are on your own.',"</js>,
1691    *          <js>"tags:[{name:'Java',description:'Java utility',externalDocs:{description:'Home page',url:'http://juneau.apache.org'}}],"</js>,
1692    *          <js>"externalDocs:{description:'Home page',url:'http://juneau.apache.org'}"</js>
1693    *       })
1694    *    )
1695    * </p>
1696    *
1697    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1698    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Swagger}
1699    * </ul>
1700    *
1701    * @return The annotation value.
1702    */
1703   Swagger swagger() default @Swagger;
1704
1705   /**
1706    * Swagger provider.
1707    *
1708    * @return The annotation value.
1709    */
1710   Class<? extends SwaggerProvider> swaggerProvider() default SwaggerProvider.Void.class;
1711
1712   /**
1713    * OpenAPI 3.1 provider.
1714    *
1715    * <p>
1716    * Sibling of {@link #swaggerProvider()} for the OpenAPI 3.1 emission path. Defaults to
1717    * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.openapi.OpenApiProvider.Void OpenApiProvider.Void}
1718    * which resolves to {@code BasicOpenApiProvider} unless the bean store provides an override.
1719    *
1720    * @return The annotation value.
1721    */
1722   Class<? extends OpenApiProvider> openApiProvider() default OpenApiProvider.Void.class;
1723
1724   /**
1725    * Optional servlet title.
1726    *
1727    * <p>
1728    * It is used to populate the Swagger title field.
1729    *
1730    * <h5 class='section'>Inheritance Rules</h5>
1731    * <ul>
1732    *    <li>Label is searched for in child-to-parent order.
1733    * </ul>
1734    *
1735    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1736    *    <li class='note'>
1737    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1738    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1739    *    <li class='note'>
1740    *       Corresponds to the swagger field <c>/info/title</c>.
1741    * </ul>
1742    *
1743    * @return The annotation value.
1744    */
1745   String[] title() default {};
1746
1747   /**
1748    * Resource authority path.
1749    *
1750    * <p>
1751    * Overrides the authority path value for this resource and any child resources.
1752    *
1753    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1754    *    <li class='note'>
1755    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1756    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1757    * </ul>
1758    *
1759    * @return The annotation value.
1760    */
1761   String uriAuthority() default "";
1762
1763   /**
1764    * Resource context path.
1765    *
1766    * <p>
1767    * Overrides the context path value for this resource and any child resources.
1768    *
1769    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1770    *    <li class='note'>
1771    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1772    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1773    * </ul>
1774    *
1775    * @return The annotation value.
1776    */
1777   String uriContext() default "";
1778
1779   /**
1780    * URI-resolution relativity.
1781    *
1782    * <p>
1783    * Specifies how relative URIs should be interpreted by serializers.
1784    *
1785    * <p>
1786    * See {@link UriResolution} for possible values.
1787    *
1788    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1789    *    <li class='note'>
1790    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1791    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1792    * </ul>
1793    *
1794    * @return The annotation value.
1795    */
1796   String uriRelativity() default "";
1797
1798   /**
1799    * URI-resolution.
1800    *
1801    * <p>
1802    * Specifies how relative URIs should be interpreted by serializers.
1803    *
1804    * <p>
1805    * See {@link UriResolution} for possible values.
1806    *
1807    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1808    *    <li class='note'>
1809    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1810    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1811    * </ul>
1812    *
1813    * @return The annotation value.
1814    */
1815   String uriResolution() default "";
1816}
